callRationale: Started with a docs.firecrawl.dev-constrained firecrawl_search call using chunks to discover official Search API evidence for categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs.
Followed up with firecrawl_scrape on the canonical Search API docs URL because the search chunks repeated request examples and only partially exposed the body-field descriptions.
strengths: The provider honored the includeDomains source policy and returned only docs.firecrawl.dev evidence.
The canonical Search API scrape contained all four expected fields, defaults, and explanatory text needed for a supported answer.
The run surfaced a useful nuance in the official docs: categories are described in the schema as category objects while an example uses string category names.
weaknesses: The first search call returned duplicated chunks and multilingual/CLI results in addition to the canonical English API page, so exact-page scraping was necessary.
The search chunk extraction alone did not capture the full body parameter descriptions for timeout and ignoreInvalidURLs.
notableFailures: One harness invocation failed before reaching the provider because the args file had been created in the wrong working directory; the file was trashed and recreated under the benchmark workdir.
The constrained search retrieval was source-policy compliant but not clean enough by itself for a complete final answer.
